### Post by Boule on 2007-02-16
Hi,

I just installed ubuntu on my laptop (dell, intel video card). The only resolution available is 1024x765, and I would really like to use 1280X800 since my laptop is widescreen (15.4").

Here's a copy of my xorg.conf file. As u can see, the only resolution is the want I want, but for some reason it's not accessible in preferences->resolution !

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"

EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-16
Your fix is probably to install the package 915resolution.   

Using Synaptic Package Manager search for "915" and you should see 915resolution. If you can't find it, then you will need to check the repositories selected. I think 915resolution is in the “universe” repository.
Easiest thing to do next is just to reboot. If you are more adventurous (and know to use Ctrl + Alt+F1 to get a command prompt to reboot out of difficulty):
[LIST]
[*]Use Alt+F2 to bring up the run command window. 
[*]Run the command: sudo /etc/sbin/915resolution
[*]Close everything that you want to save 
[*]restart X windows "Ctrl + Alt + Bksp"
[/LIST]
The default setting will probably work.  If not, then the card has not managed to interrogate the screen correctly.  You need to edit the file /etc/default/915resolution. Instructions are in the file.  Leave: mode=auto but set the resolution parameters for the resolution you are after.

---

### Post by Boule on 2007-02-16
I c...
I found  than 915 solution googling for my problem, but it was for sony laptops, and the link given showed that very few laptops were supported...
Why doesn't it just read the resolution from xorg.conf, as it's supposed to ?
Will I have problems to install aiglx/beryl ?

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-16
As I understand it, the intel graphics bios is the problem, it needs to be forced to check the relevant resolutions for your screen and save them in it's temporary storage.  Once they are there they are available to the driver to use - I think the idea is to include it in the next version of the i810 driver. 

I'm giving out this information about once a day!

---
